DATASOURCE::CS::ClusterUserKubeconfig.description: DATASOURCE::CS::ClusterUserKubeconfig is used to query the kubeconfig file of a Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) cluster by cluster ID.

ClusterId.description: The ID of the ACK cluster.

PrivateIpAddress.description:
Specifies whether to obtain the credential that is used to connect to the cluster over the internal network. Valid values:
true: obtains the credential that is used to connect to the cluster over the internal network.
false: obtains the credential that is used to connect to the cluster over the Internet.
Default value: false.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

TemporaryDurationMinutes.description:
The validity period of a temporary kubeconfig file. Unit: minutes. Valid values: 15 to 4320.
Note If you do not specify this parameter, the system automatically specifies a validity period, which is indicated by the expiration field in the response.

ClusterId.description: The ID of the ACK cluster.

Config.description: The content of the kubeconfig file.

Expiration.description: The expiration time of the kubeconfig file. The value is the UTC time displayed in RFC3339 format.

DATASOURCE::CS::KubernetesCluster.description: DATASOURCE::CS::KubernetesCluster is used to query the information about a Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) cluster.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ClusterSpec.description:
The type of the managed Kubernetes cluster. This parameter is returned for a managed Kubernetes cluster. Valid values:
- ack.pro.small: professional managed Kubernetes cluster.
- ack.standard: standard managed Kubernetes cluster.

ClusterType.description:
The type of the cluster. Valid values:
- Kubernetes: dedicated Kubernetes cluster
- ManagedKubernetes: managed Kubernetes cluster
- Ask: ASK cluster
- ExternalKubernetes: registered external Kubernetes cluster

Created.description: The time when the cluster was created.

CurrentVersion.description: The current Kubernetes version of the cluster. For more information about the Kubernetes versions supported by ACK, see Release notes for Kubernetes versions.

DeletionProtection.description:
Indicates whether deletion protection is enabled. If deletion protection is enabled, the cluster cannot be deleted in the ACK console or by calling the API. Valid values:
- true: Deletion protection is enabled. You cannot delete the cluster in the ACK console or by calling the API.
- false: Deletion protection is not enabled. You can delete the cluster in the ACK console or by calling the API.

DockerVersion.description: The Docker version that is used by the cluster.

ExternalLoadbalancerId.description: The ID of the Server Load Balancer (SLB) instance that is used for the Ingress of the cluster.

InitVersion.description: The Kubernetes version that is initially used by the cluster.

MaintenanceWindow.description: The maintenance window of the cluster. This feature is available in only professional managed Kubernetes clusters.

MasterUrl.description: The address of the cluster. It includes an internal endpoint and a public endpoint.

Name.description: The name of the cluster. The name must be 1 to 63 characters in length, and can contain digits, letters, and hyphens (-). It cannot start with a hyphen (-).

NetworkMode.description:
The network mode of the cluster. Valid values: 
- classic: the classic network 
- vpc: virtual private cloud (VPC) 
- overlay: overlay network
- calico: network powered by Calico
Default value: vpc.

NextVersion.description: The Kubernetes version to which the cluster can be upgraded.

Parameters.description: A collection of cluster ROS parameters.

PrivateZone.description:
Indicates whether Alibaba Cloud DNS PrivateZone is enabled.
- true: indicates that Alibaba Cloud DNS PrivateZone is enabled.- false: indicates that Alibaba Cloud DNS PrivateZone is not enabled.

Profile.description:
Indicates the scenario in which the cluster is used. Valid values:
- Default: indicates that the cluster is used in non-edge computing scenarios.
- Edge: indicates that the ACK cluster is used in edge computing scenarios.

RegionId.description: The ID of the region where the cluster is deployed.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the cluster belongs.

SecurityGroupId.description: The ID of the security group to which the instances of the cluster belong.

Size.description: The number of nodes in the cluster. Master nodes and worker nodes are included.

State.description:
The state of the cluster. Valid values:
- initial: The cluster is being created.
- failed: The cluster failed to be created.
- running: The cluster is running.
- updating: The cluster is being upgraded.
- updating_failed: The cluster failed to be upgraded.
- scaling: The cluster is being scaled.
- waiting: The registered cluster is waiting for connecting.
- disconnected: The registeredcluster is disconnected.
- stopped: The cluster is stopped.
- deleting: The cluster is being deleted.
- deleted: The cluster is deleted.
- delete_failed: The cluster failed to be deleted.

SubnetCidr.description:
The pod CIDR block. It must be a valid and private CIDR block, and must be one of the following CIDR blocks or their subnets:
- 10.0.0.0/8
- 172.16-31.0.0/12-16
- 192.168.0.0/16
The pod CIDR block cannot overlap with that of the VPC or those of the ACK clusters that are deployed in the VPC. For more information about the network segmentation of ACK clusters, see Plan CIDR blocks for ACK clusters in a VPC.

Tags.description: The labels of the cluster.

Updated.description: The time when the cluster was updated.

VSwitchId.description: The IDs of the vSwitches. You can select one to three vSwitches when you create an ACK cluster. vSwitches in different zones are recommended to ensure high availability.

VSwitchIds.description: The list of cluster control plane VSwitch ID.

VpcId.description: The ID of the VPC where the cluster is deployed. This parameter is required when you create an ACK cluster.

WorkerRamRoleName.description:
The name of the worker RAM role. 
The RAM role is assigned to the worker nodes of the cluster and allows the worker nodes to manage Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances.

ZoneId.description: The ID of the zone where the cluster is deployed.

DATASOURCE::CS::KubernetesClusters.description: DATASOURCE::CS::KubernetesClusters is used to query Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) clusters.

ClusterSpec.description:
The specification of the cluster. If you set the cluster type to ManagedKubernetes, you can use cluster specifications to distinguish clusters. Valid values:
ack.pro.small: professional managed Kubernetes cluster
ack.standard: standard managed Kubernetes cluster
By default, this parameter is empty. This indicates that the parameter is not used to filter clusters.

ClusterType.description:
The type of the cluster. Valid values:
Kubernetes: dedicated Kubernetes cluster
ManagedKubernetes: managed Kubernetes cluster, serverless Kubernetes (ASK) cluster, or edge Kubernetes cluster
Ask: ASK cluster
ExternalKubernetes: registered external cluster
When you query an ASK cluster, the value specified when the cluster was created is returned.

PrimaryRegion.description: The initial source region of the replication group.

PrimaryZone.description: The initial source available area of the replication group.

RPO.description: The RPO value set by the consistency group in seconds. Currently only 900 seconds are supported.

Site.description: Site information sources for replication pairs and consistent replication groups.

SourceRegionId.description: The ID of the region to which the production site belongs.

SourceZoneId.description: The ID of the zone to which the production site belongs.

StandbyRegion.description: The initial destination region of the replication group.

StandbyZone.description: The initial destination zone of the replication group.

Tags.description: The tags of the replication pair-consistent group.

Description.description: The description of the replication pair.

DestinationDiskId.description: The ID of the secondary disk.

Default is Never.

CreateTime.description: The time when the activation code was created.

DeregisteredCount.description: The number of instances that have been logged out.

Description.description: The description of the activation code.

Disabled.description: Indicates whether the activation code is disabled.

InstanceCount.description: The maximum number of times that the activation code can be used to register managed instances.

InstanceName.description: The default prefix of the instance name.

IpAddressRange.description: The IP address of the host that allows the activation code to be used.

RegisteredCount.description: The number of instances that were registered.

TimeToLiveInHours.description: The validity period of the activation code. Unit: hours.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the automatic snapshot policy belongs.

Tags.description: Tags of snapshotpolicy.

AutoSnapshotPolicies.description: The list of auto snapshot policies.

AutoSnapshotPolicyIds.description: The list of auto snapshot policy ids.

DATASOURCE::ECS::AutoSnapshotPolicy.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::AutoSnapshotPolicy is used to query an automatic snapshot policy.

AutoSnapshotPolicyId.description: The name of the automatic snapshot policy.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

AutoSnapshotPolicyName.description: Name of the automatic snapshot policy.

CreateTime.description: The time when the automatic snapshot policy was created. The time follows the ISO 8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

DiskNums.description: Number of cloud disks with this policy enabled.

RepeatWeekdays.description: The days of the week on which to create automatic snapshots. Valid values: 1 to 7, which correspond to the days of the week. 1 indicates Monday. One or more days can be specified.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group.

RetentionDays.description:
The retention period of the automatic snapshot. Unit: days. Valid values:
- -1: The automatic snapshot is retained until it is deleted.
- 1 to 65536: The automatic snapshot is retained for the specified number of days.

Tags.description: The tags of the automatic snapshot policy.

TimePoints.description: The time when the automatic snapshot policy was created. The time follows the ISO 8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

VolumeNums.description: The number of extended volumes to which the automatic snapshot policy is applied.

- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

CommandContent.description: Command content, encoded in Base64 and transmitted.

CreateTime.description: Command creation time.

Description.description: Command description.

Tags.description: The label information of the command.

Type.description: Command type.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Commands.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Commands is used to query all available commands that you created.

CommandName.description: The name of the command. Partial command names are not supported.

Description.description: Command description.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Type.description:
The command type. Valid values:
RunBatScript: batch command, applicable to Windows instances
RunPowerShellScript: PowerShell command, applicable to Windows instances
RunShellScript: shell command, applicable to Linux instances

The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

DedicatedHostClusterName.description: The name of the dedicated host cluster.

Description.description: The description of the dedicated host cluster.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the dedicated host cluster belongs.

Tags.description: The tags of the dedicated host cluster.

ZoneId.description: The zone ID of the dedicated host cluster.

DATASOURCE::ECS::DedicatedHostClusters.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::DedicatedHostClusters is used to query the information about dedicated host clusters.

DedicatedHostClusterName.description: The name of the dedicated host cluster.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description:
The ID of the resource group to which the dedicated host cluster belongs. 
If this parameter is specified to query resources,
up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

ZoneId.description:
The zone ID of the dedicated host cluster. 
You can call the DescribeZones operation to query the most recent zone list.

DedicatedHostClusterIds.description: The list of dedicated host cluster IDs.

DedicatedHostClusters.description: The list of dedicated host clusters.

DATASOURCE::ECS::DedicatedHosts.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::DedicatedHosts is used to query the information about dedicated hosts.

DedicatedHostClusterId.description: The ID of the dedicated host cluster.

DedicatedHostIds.description: The IDs of the dedicated hosts. You can specify up to 100 dedicated host IDs.

DedicatedHostName.description: The name of the dedicated host.

DedicatedHostType.description: The type of the dedicated host.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the dedicated host belongs.

Status.description:
The service status of the dedicated host. Valid values:
Available: The dedicated host is running normally.
UnderAssessment: The dedicated host is at risk, which may cause issues to ECS instances on the dedicated host.
 PermanentFailure: A permanent failure exists and the dedicated host is unavailable.
Default value: Available.

Tags.description: Tags of ddh.

ZoneId.description: The zone ID of the dedicated host.

DedicatedHostIds.description: The list of dedicated host ids.

DATASOURCE::ECS::DeploymentSet.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::DeploymentSet is used to query the information about a deployment set.

The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

CreateTime.description: The time when the deployment set was created.

DeploymentSetName.description: The name of the deployment set.

GroupCount.description: The number of deployment set groups in the deployment set.

InstanceAmount.description: The number of instances in the deployment set.

InstanceIds.description: The IDs of the instances in the deployment set.

DATASOURCE::ECS::DeploymentSets.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::DeploymentSets is used to query the information about deployment sets.

DeploymentSetIds.description: he IDs of deployment sets. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 deployment set IDs in the format of ["ds-xxxxxxxxx", "ds-yyyyyyyyy", … "ds-zzzzzzzzz"]. Separate multiple deployment set IDs with commas (,).

DeploymentSetName.description: The name of the deployment set.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Strategy.description:
The deployment strategy. Valid values:
Availability: high availability strategy.
 AvailabilityGroup: high availability group strategy.

DeploymentSetIds.description: the list of deployment set ids

DATASOURCE::ECS::Disk.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Disk is used to query the information about a cloud disk.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

BurstingEnabled.description: Does the data disk turn on Burst (performance Burst).

Category.description: Disk type.

CreateTime.description: The creation time.

DeleteAutoSnapshot.description: Whether to delete automatic snapshots at the same time.

DeleteWithInstance.description: Whether to release with the instance.

Description.description: The description.

Device.description: Cloud disk or the device name of the mounted instance on the site.

EnableAutoSnapshot.description: Whether the disk implements an automatic snapshot policy.

EnableAutomatedSnapshotPolicy.description: Whether the disk implements an automatic snapshot policy.

ExpiredTime.description: The expiration time of a monthly disk.

Iops.description: Number of read/write (I/O) operations per second.

MountInstanceNum.description: Number of instances mounted on shared storage.

MultiAttach.description: Whether to enable the multi-Mount feature.

PaymentType.description: Payment method for disk.

PerformanceLevel.description: Performance levels of ESSD cloud disk.

ProductCode.description: The product logo of the cloud market.

ProvisionedIops.description: The preconfigured read and write IOPS of the ESSD AutoPL cloud disk. Possible values: 0 ~ min{50,000, 1000 * capacity-baseline performance}.

ResourceGroupId.description: The resource group id.

StorageClusterId.description: The ID of the dedicated block storage cluster. If you need to create a cloud disk in the specified dedicated block storage cluster, specify this parameter.

StorageSetId.description: The ID of the Save set.

StorageSetPartitionNumber.description: Number of Save set partitions. Value range: greater than or equal to 2. The maximum value cannot exceed the equity quota limit displayed after calling.

Tags.description: The tags.

ZoneId.description: ID of the free zone to which the disk belongs.

DATASOURCE::ECS::DiskCategories.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::DiskCategories is used to query the information about disk categories.

DataDiskCategory.description:
The category of the data disk. Valid values: 
cloud: basic disk
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
ephemeral_ssd: local SSD
cloud_essd: ESSD

InstanceType.description:
The instance type. For more information,see Instance families or call the DescribeInstanceTypes,operation to query the most recent instance type list.
When the Type parameter is set to system or data,you must set the InstanceType parameter.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

SystemDiskCategory.description:
The category of the system disk. Valid values: 
cloud: basic disk
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
ephemeral_ssd: local SSD
cloud_essd: ESSD

Type.description:
The resource type to query.
If you set Type to data,you can specify the InstanceType parameter to disk.
If you set Type to system, you must specify the InstanceType parameter.
Valid values:
system: system disk
data: data disk

ZoneId.description: The ID of the zone for which to query resources.

DiskCategoryIds.description: The list of disk category IDs.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Disks.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Disks is used to query the Elastic Block Storage (EBS) devices that you created, including cloud disks and local disks.

AdditionalAttributes.description: The value of attribute N. Set the value to IOPS, which indicates the maximum IOPS of the disk.

AutoSnapshotPolicyId.description: The ID of the automatic snapshot policy that is applied to the cloud disk.

Category.description:
The category of the disk. Valid values:
all: all disk categories
cloud: basic disk
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
ephemeral: retired local disk
ephemeral_ssd: local SSD
cloud_essd: ESSD
local_ssd_pro: I/O-intensive local disk
local_hdd_pro: throughput-intensive local disk

DeleteAutoSnapshot.description:
Specifies whether to delete the automatic snapshots of the cloud disk when the disk is released.
Default value: false.

DeleteWithInstance.description:
Specifies whether to release the cloud disk when its associated instance is released. Valid values:
true: The cloud disk is released when its associated instance is released.
false: The cloud disk is not released but is retained as a pay-as-you-go data disk when its associated instance is released.
Default value: false.

DiskChargeType.description:
The billing method of the disk. Valid values:
PrePaid: subscription
PostPaid: pay-as-you-go

DiskIds.description:
The IDs of disks.
The value is a JSON array that consists of up to 100 disk IDs.
Separate the disk IDs with commas (,).

DiskType.description:
The type of the disk. Valid values:
all: system disk and data disk
system: system disk
data: data disk
Default value: all.

EnableAutoSnapshot.description:
Specifies whether the automatic snapshot policy feature is enabled for the cloud disk.
true: The automatic snapshot policy feature is enabled for the cloud disk.
false: The automatic snapshot policy feature is disabled for the cloud disk.
Note By default, the automatic snapshot policy feature is enabled for created cloud disks. You need only to apply an automatic snapshot policy to a cloud disk before you can use the automatic snapshot policy.

EnableAutomatedSnapshotPolicy.description:
Specifies whether an automatic snapshot policy is applied to the cloud disk.
true: An automatic snapshot policy is applied to the cloud disk.
false: No automatic snapshot policy is applied to the cloud disk.
Default value: false.

EnableShared.description: Specifies whether the disk is a Shared Block Storage device.

Encrypted.description:
Specifies whether to query only encrypted cloud disks.
Default value: false.

InstanceId.description: The ID of the instance to which the disk is attached.

KMSKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used by the cloud disk.

MultiAttach.description:
Specifies whether the multi-attach feature is enabled for the disk. Valid values:
Disabled: The multi-attach feature is disabled.
Enabled: The multi-attach feature is enabled.
LegacyShared: Shared Block Storage devices are queried.
The multi-attach feature is in invitational preview. To use this feature, submit a ticket.

Portable.description:
Specifies whether the disk is removable. Valid values:
true: The disk is removable.A removable disk can independently exist and can be attached to or detached from an instance within the same zone.
false: The disk is not removable. A disk that is not removable cannot independently exist or be attached to or detached from an instance within the same zone.
The Portable attribute of the following disks is false, and these disks share the same lifecycle with their associated instances:
Local disks
Local SSDs
Subscription data disks

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description:
The ID of the resource group to which the disk belongs. 
If this parameter is specified to query resources,up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SnapshotId.description: The ID of the snapshot used to create the cloud disk.

Status.description:
The state of the cloud disk. For more information, see Disk states. Valid values:
In_use
Available
Attaching
Detaching
Creating
ReIniting
All
Default value: All.

Tags.description: Tags of disks.

ZoneId.description: The ID of the zone for which to query resources.

DATASOURCE::ECS::HpcCluster.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::HpcCluster is used to query a High Performance Computing (HPC) cluster.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Description.description: The description of the HPC cluster.

HpcClusterName.description: The name of the HPC cluster.

DATASOURCE::ECS::HpcClusters.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::HpcClusters is used to query available High Performance Computing (HPC) clusters.

HpcClusterIds.description: The IDs of the HPC clusters. You can specify up to 100 HPC cluster IDs.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

HpcClusterIds.description: the list of hpc cluster ids

DATASOURCE::ECS::Images.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Images is used to query available images.

ActionType.description:
The scenario in which the image is used. Default value: CreateEcs. Valid values:
CreateEcs: instance creation
ChangeOS: replacement of the system disk or operating system

Architecture.description:
The image architecture. Valid values:
i38
x86_64
arm64

ImageFamily.description:
The name of the image family. You can set this parameter to query images of the specified image family.
This parameter is empty by default.

ImageName.description: The name of the image. Support for fuzzy queries using *.

ImageOwnerAlias.description:
The source of the image. Valid values:
system: public images provided by Alibaba Cloud.
self: your custom images.
others: shared images from other Alibaba Cloud accounts, or community images published by other Alibaba Cloud accounts. Take note of the following items:
- To query community images, you must set IsPublic to true.
To query shared images, you must set IsPublic to false or leave the IsPublic parameter empty.
marketplace: Alibaba Cloud Marketplace images. If Alibaba Cloud Marketplace images are returned in the response, you can use the images without subscription. You must pay attention to the billing details of Alibaba Cloud Marketplace images.
This parameter is empty by default, which indicates that the results that match system, self, and others are returned.

InstanceType.description: The instance type for which the image can be used.

IsPublic.description:
Specifies whether to query published community images. Valid values:
true: queries published community images. When you set this parameter to true, you must set ImageOwnerAlias to others.
false: queries other image types than community images. The specific image types to be queried are determined by the ImageOwnerAlias value.
Default value: false.

IsSupportCloudinit.description: Specifies whether the image supports cloud-init.

IsSupportIoOptimized.description: Specifies whether the image can be used on I/O optimized instances.

OSType.description:
The operating system type of the image. Valid values:
windows
linux

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the custom image belongs. If this parameter is specified to query resources, up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SnapshotId.description: The ID of the snapshot used to create the custom image.

Status.description:
The state of the image. Default value: Available. Valid values:
Creating: The image is being created.
Waiting: The image is waiting to be processed.
Available: The image is available.
UnAvailable: The image is unavailable.
CreateFailed: The image failed to be created.
Deprecated: The image is discontinued.
You can specify multiple values. Separate multiple values with commas (,).

Tags.description: Tags of image.

Usage.description:
Specifies whether the image is running on an Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instance. Valid values:
instance: The image is already in use and running on an ECS instance.
none: The image is not in use.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Instance.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Instance is used to query the information about an Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instance.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ActionOnMaintenance.description: The O&M action attribute of the instance.

AutoRebootTime.description: Automatic reboot time after instance configuration change.

AutoReleaseTime.description: The time when to automatically release the pay-as-you-go instance.

AutoRenewEnabled.description: Whether automatic renewal has been set.

ClusterId.description: The ID of the cluster to which the instance belongs.

CreateTime.description: The create time.

CreditSpecification.description: The performance mode of the burstable instance.

DedicatedHostAttribute.description: Details about the dedicated host. It is an array that consists of the DedicatedHostClusterId, DedicatedHostId, and DedicatedHostName parameters.

DedicatedInstanceAttribute.description: The attributes of the instance on the dedicated host.

DeletionProtection.description: The release protection attribute of the instance. This parameter indicates whether you can use the ECS console or call the DeleteInstance operation to release the instance.

DeploymentSetGroupNo.description: The number of the deployment set group to which the instance belongs in a deployment set.

DeploymentSetId.description: The ID of the deployment set in which the instance is deployed.

Description.description: The description of the instance.

DeviceAvailable.description: Indicates whether data disks can be attached to the instance.

EcsCapacityReservationAttr.description: Details about the capacity reservation related to the instance.

EipAddress.description: Details about the EIP associated with the instance.

ExpiredTime.description: The time when the instance expires. The time follows the ISO 8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

GpuAmount.description: The number of GPUs for the instance type.

GpuSpec.description: The category of GPUs for the instance type.

HpcClusterId.description: ID of the HPC cluster to which the instance belongs.

InstanceName.description: The name of the instance. Fuzzy search with the asterisk (*) wildcard characters is supported.

InstanceNetworkType.description: The network type of the instance.

InstanceType.description: The instance type of the instance.

InstanceTypeFamily.description: The instance family of the instance.

InternetChargeType.description: The billing method for network usage.

InternetMaxBandwidthIn.description: The maximum inbound public bandwidth. Unit: Mbit/s.

InternetMaxBandwidthOut.description: The maximum outbound public bandwidth. Unit: Mbit/s.

IoOptimized.description: Specifies whether the instance is I/O optimized.

KeyPairName.description: The name of the SSH key pair bound to the instance.

LocalStorageAmount.description: The number of local disks attached to the instance.

LocalStorageCapacity.description: The capacity of local disks attached to the instance.

MaintenanceWindows.description: The list of operations window instances.

Memory.description: The memory size of the instance. Unit: MiB.

NetworkInterfaces.description: Details about the ENIs bound to the instance.

NotifyOnMaintenance.description: Whether to send an event notification before the instance goes down.

OperationLocks.description: The reasons why the instance was locked.

OsName.description: The name of the operating system of the instance.

OsNameEn.description: The English name of the operating system of the instance.

OsType.description: The type of the operating system of the instance.

PaymentType.description: The billing method of the instance.

PeriodUnit.description: Renewal unit.

PrivatePoolOptionsId.description: Private pool ID. When the return value is open, the private pool ID is the private pool ID assigned when the system automatically matches.

PrivatePoolOptionsMatchCriteria.description: The private pool matching pattern of the instance.

PublicIpAddress.description: The public IP addresses of the instance.

RdmaIpAddress.description: The RDMA network IP of the HPC instance.

Recyclable.description: Indicates whether the instance can be recycled.

RenewalStatus.description: The automatic renewal status of the instance.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the instance belongs. If this parameter is specified to query resources, up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SecurityGroupIds.description: The IDs of the security groups to which the instance belongs.

SerialNumber.description: The serial number of the instance.

SpotDuration.description: The protection period of the preemptible instance. Unit: hours.

SpotPriceLimit.description: The maximum hourly price of the instance. It can be accurate to three decimal places. This parameter is valid only when the SpotStrategy parameter is set to SpotWithPriceLimit.

SpotStrategy.description: The bidding policy for the pay-as-you-go instance.

StartTime.description: The time when the instance was last started. The time follows the ISO 8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

StoppedMode.description: Indicates whether the instance continues to be billed after it is stopped.

Tags.description: The tags of the instance.

UserData.description: The custom data of the instance.

VpcAttributes.description: The VPC attributes of the instance.

ZoneId.description: The zone ID of the instance.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Instances.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Instances is used to query the information about Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances.

AdditionalAttributes.description:
The value of attribute N. Valid values of N: 1 to 20. Valid values:
META_OPTIONS: instance metadata
DDH_CLUSTER: dedicated host cluster
NETWORK_PRIMARY_ENI_IP: secondary IP address associated with the primary ENI of the instance

EipAddresses.description: The elastic IP addresses (EIPs) of instances. This parameter is valid when InstanceNetworkType is set to vpc. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 IP addresses. Separate the IP addresses with commas (,).

HpcClusterId.description: The ID of the High Performance Computing (HPC) cluster to which the instance belongs.

InnerIpAddresses.description: The internal IP addresses of instances located in the classic network. This parameter is valid when InstanceNetworkType is set to classic. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 IP addresses. Separate the IP addresses with commas (,).

InstanceChargeType.description:
The billing method of the instance. Valid values:
PostPaid: pay-as-you-go
PrePaid: subscription

InstanceId.description: The IDs of instances. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 instance IDs. Separate the instance IDs with commas (,).

InstanceIds.description: The IDs of instances. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 instance IDs. Separate the instance IDs with commas (,).

InstanceName.description: The name of the instance. Fuzzy search with the asterisk (*) wildcard is supported.

InstanceNetworkType.description:
The network type of the instance. Valid values:
classic: classic network
vpc: VPC

InstanceType.description: The instance type of the instance.

InstanceTypeFamily.description: The instance family of the instance.

InternetChargeType.description:
The billing method for network usage. Valid values:
PayByBandwidth: pay-by-bandwidth
PayByTraffic: pay-by-traffic

IoOptimized.description: Specifies whether the instance is I/O optimized.

Ipv6Address.description: IPv6 address N of the elastic network interface (ENI). You can specify multiple IPv6 addresses. Valid values of N: 1 to 100.

KeyPairName.description: The name of the SSH key pair bound to the instance.

PrivateIpAddresses.description: The private IP addresses of instances located in VPCs. This parameter is valid when InstanceNetworkType is set to vpc. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 IP addresses. Separate the IP addresses with commas (,).

PublicIpAddresses.description: The public IP addresses of instances. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 IP addresses. Separate the IP addresses with commas (,).

RdmaIpAddresses.description: The Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) IP addresses of HPC instances.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description:
The ID of the resource group to which the instance belongs. 
If this parameter is specified to query resources,up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SecurityGroupId.description: The ID of the security group to which the instance belongs.

Status.description:
The state of the instance. Valid values:
Pending
Running
Starting
Stopping
Stopped

Tags.description: Tags of instance.

VSwitchId.description: The ID of the vSwitch.

VpcId.description: The ID of the virtual private cloud (VPC).

ZoneId.description: The zone ID of the instance.

InstanceIds.description: The list of InstanceIds.

DATASOURCE::ECS::KeyPair.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::KeyPair is used to query the information about a key pair.

KeyPairName.description:
The name of the key pair. You can use the asterisk (*) symbol as a wildcard in regular expressions to perform a fuzzy search for key pairs. Sample patterns:
- *SshKey: queries key pairs whose names end with SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
- SshKey*: queries key pairs whose names start with SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
- *SshKey*: queries key pairs whose names include SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
- SshKey: queries the key pair named SshKey.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

CreateTime.description: The time when the key pair was created.

FingerPrint.description: The fingerprint of the key pair.

KeyPairName.description: The name of the key pair.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group.

Tags.description: The tags of the key pair.

DATASOURCE::ECS::KeyPairs.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::KeyPairs is used to query key pairs.

KeyPairFingerPrint.description: The fingerprint of the key pair. The message-digest algorithm 5 (MD5) is used based on the public key fingerprint format defined in RFC 4716. For more information, see RFC 4716.

KeyPairName.description:
he name of the key pair. You can use the asterisk (*) symbol as a wildcard in regular expressions to query key pairs by performing a fuzzy search. Sample patterns:
*SshKey: queries key pairs whose names end with SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
SshKey*: queries key pairs whose names start with SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
*SshKey*: queries key pairs whose names include SshKey, including the key pair named SshKey.
SshKey: queries the key pair named SshKey.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the key pair belongs. If this parameter is specified to query resources, up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

Tags.description: Tags of keypair.

KeyPairNames.description: The list of key pair names.

DATASOURCE::ECS::LaunchTemplate.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::LaunchTemplate is used to query the information about a launch template.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

AutoReleaseTime.description: Automatic release time (UTC). The format is: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ.

CreateTime.description: The time when the instance launch template was created.

CreatedBy.description: The creator of the launch template.

DefaultVersionNumber.description: The default version number of the template.

DeletionProtection.description: Instance deletion protection attribute.

DeploymentSetId.description: The ID of the deployment set.

Description.description: Instance description. 2 to 256 English or Chinese characters in length and cannot start with' http:// 'or' https.

InstanceChargeType.description: The billing method of the instance.

InstanceName.description: The name of the instance. The length is 2~128 English or Chinese characters. It must start with a large or small letter or Chinese, and cannot start with' http:// 'or' https. It can contain numbers, colons (:), underscores (_), or dashes (-).

InstanceType.description: The instance type.

InternetChargeType.description: Network billing method.

InternetMaxBandwidthIn.description: The maximum public inbound bandwidth, in Mbit/s.

InternetMaxBandwidthOut.description: The maximum outbound bandwidth of the public network. The unit is Mbit/s. The value range is 0~100.

IoOptimized.description: Whether to optimize the instance for I/O.

Ipv6AddressCount.description: Specify the number of randomly generated IPv6 addresses for the primary network card. The value range is 1 to 10.

KeyPairName.description: The name of the key pair.

LatestVersionNumber.description: The latest version number of the template.

LaunchTemplateId.description: The ID of the launch template.

LaunchTemplateName.description: The name of the launch template.

ModifiedTime.description: The time when the launch template was modified.

NetworkType.description: The network type of the instance.

PasswordInherit.description: Whether to use the password preset by the image.

Period.description: The duration of the purchase of resources, in months. When creating an instance, the parameter 'InstanceChargeType' takes effect and is required only when the value of the parameter 'PrePaid' is 'PrePaid. Value range: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60.

PrivateIpAddress.description: The private IP address of the instance.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the instance, Elastic Block Storage (EBS) device, and ENI belong.

SecurityEnhancementStrategy.description: Whether to turn on security reinforcement for the operating system.

SecurityGroupIds.description: The IDs of one or more security groups to which you want to add the instance.

SpotPriceLimit.description: Set the maximum price per hour for the instance. The maximum number of 3 decimal places is supported. The value of the parameter 'SpotStrategy' is 'SpotWithPriceLimit.

SpotStrategy.description: The preemption policy for a pay-as-you-go instance. This parameter takes effect when the value of the parameter 'InstanceChargeType' is 'PostPaid.

Tags.description: The tags of the launch template.

TemplateTag.description: The tags of the launch template.

UserData.description: The user-defined data of the instance must be encoded in Base64. The maximum original data is 16KB.

VSwitchId.description: The ID of the vSwitch.

VersionDescription.description: Instance launch template version description. 2 to 256 English or Chinese characters in length and cannot start with' http:// 'or' https.

VpcId.description: VPC Id.

ZoneId.description: The ID of the zone to which the instance belongs.

DATASOURCE::ECS::LaunchTemplates.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::LaunchTemplates is used to query launch templates.

LaunchTemplateId.description: The ID of the launch template.

LaunchTemplateName.description: The name of the launch template.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

TemplateResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the enterprise resource group to which the template is launched.

LaunchTemplateIds.description: The list of launch template IDs.

DATASOURCE::ECS::ManagedInstances.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::ManagedInstances is used to query managed instances.

ActivationId.description: The ID of the activation code

InstanceId.description: The ID of managed instance. Valid values of N: 1 to 50.

InstanceIp.description: The internal or public IP address of the managed instance

InstanceName.description: The name of the managed instance

OsType.description:
The operating system type of the managed instance.  Valid values:
windows
linux

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Tags.description: Tags of managedinstance.

InstanceIds.description: The list of managed instance ids.

DATASOURCE::ECS::NetworkInterfaces.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::NetworkInterfaces is used to query the information about elastic network interfaces (ENIs).

InstanceId.description: The ID of the instance to which the ENI is bound.

Ipv6Addresses.description: IPv6 address N of the ENI. You can specify multiple IPv6 addresses. Valid values of N: 1 to 100.

NetworkInterfaceIds.description: The ID of ENI N. Valid values of N: 1 to 100.

PrimaryIpAddress.description: The primary private IPv4 address of the ENI.

PrivateIpAddresses.description: Secondary private IPv4 address N of the ENI. Valid values of N: 1 to 100.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description:
The ID of the resource group to which the eni belongs. 
If this parameter is specified to query resources,up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SecurityGroupId.description:
The ID of the security group to which the secondary ENI belongs.
To query the details of secondary ENIs based on the ID of a security group, specify this parameter.
To query the details of primary ENIs based on the ID of a security group, call the DescribeInstances operation and specify the SecurityGroupId parameter.

ServiceManaged.description: Specifies whether the user is an Alibaba Cloud service or a distributor.

Status.description:
The state of the ENI. Valid values:
Creating: The ENI is being created.
Available: The ENI is not bound to an instance.
Attaching: The ENI is being bound to an instance.
InUse: The ENI is bound to an instance.
Detaching: The ENI is being unbound from an instance.
Deleting: The ENI is being deleted.
CreateFailed: The ENI cannot be created.
This parameter is empty by default, which indicates that ENIs in all states are queried.

Tags.description: Tags of eni.

Type.description:
The type of the ENI. Valid values:
Primary
Secondary
This parameter is empty by default, which indicates that both primary and secondary ENIs are queried.

VSwitchId.description: The ID of the vSwitch to which the ENI is connected.

VpcId.description: The ID of the virtual private cloud (VPC) to which the ENI belongs.

DATASOURCE::ECS::RecommendInstanceTypes.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::RecommendInstanceTypes is used to query the recommended instance types of Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances.

Cores.description:
The number of vCPUs of the instance.
Note If the Cores and Memory parameters are both specified, all instance types that offer the vCPUs and memory size specified by the parameters are matched.

InstanceChargeType.description:
The billing method of the instances of the instance type. Valid values:
PrePaid: subscription
PostPaid: pay-as-you-go
Default value: PostPaid.

InstanceFamilyLevel.description: The level of the instance family. Valid values: EntryLevel, EnterpriseLevel, CreditEntryLevel.

InstanceType.description:
The specified instance type. For more information, see Instance families or call ECS DescribeInstanceTypes operation to query the most recent instance type list.
Note If the InstanceType parameter is specified, none of the Cores and Memory parameters can be specified.

InstanceTypeFamilies.description: The list of instance family to be filtered out. You can also call ECS DescribeInstanceTypeFamilies operation to query the list of instance families.

IoOptimized.description:
Specifies whether to match I/O optimized instances. The IoOptimized parameter cannot be specified when the instance is not I/O optimized. Valid values:
optimized: matches I/O optimized instances.
none: matches non-I/O optimized instances.
Default value: optimized.
If you query alternative instance types for retired instance types, this parameter is set to none by default. 
Default value: none.

MaxPrice.description:
The maximum hourly price for pay-as-you-go instances or preemptible instances.
Note If this parameter is specified, the SpotStrategy parameter must be set to SpotWithPriceLimit.

Memory.description:
The memory size of the instance. Unit: GiB.
Note If the Cores and Memory parameters are both specified, all instance types that offer the vCPUs and memory size specified by the parameters are matched.

PriorityStrategy.description:
The policy for recommending instance types. Valid values:
InventoryFirst: Instance types are recommended in descending order based on resource availability.
PriceFirst: Instance types are recommended in ascending order based on hourly price per vCPU.
NewProductFirst: The latest instance types are recommended first.
Default value: InventoryFirst.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Scene.description:
Specifies the scenario in which the instance type is recommended. Valid values:
UPGRADE: instance type upgrade or downgrade
CREATE: instance creation
Default value: CREATE.

SpotStrategy.description:
The bidding policy of preemptible instances. Valid values:
NoSpot: applies to regular pay-as-you-go instances.
SpotWithPriceLimit: applies to preemptible instances that have user-defined maximum hourly prices.
SpotAsPriceGo: applies to preemptible instances that are of the market price at the time of purchase.
Note If the SpotStrategy parameter is specified, the InstanceChargeType parameter must be set to PostPaid.
Default value: NoSpot.

SystemDiskCategory.description:
The category of the system disk. Valid values:
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
cloud_essd: enhanced SSD (ESSD)
cloud: basic disk
For non-I/O optimized instances, the default value is cloud.
For I/O optimized instances, the default value is cloud_efficiency.

ZoneId.description: The zone ID of the alternative instance types. You can call ECS DescribeZones operation to query the most recent zone list.When you specify this parameter, we recommend that you set ZoneMatchMode to the default value Include. This value indicates that instance types in the zone specified by ZoneId are preferentially recommended, and instance types in other zones in the same region are also listed.

ZoneMatchMode.description:
Specifies which alternative instance types are recommended. Valid values:
Strict: recommends only alternative instance types in the zone specified by ZoneId.
Include: recommends all instance types in all the zones in the same region as the specified instance type.
When ZoneId is specified, the default value of this parameter is Strict. This value indicates that only alternative instance types in the zone specified by ZoneId are recommended.

InstanceTypeIds.description: The list of instance type ids. Note that instance type ids are not unique.

InstanceTypes.description: The list of instance types, including information such as cores and memory.

DATASOURCE::ECS::SecurityGroup.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::SecurityGroup is used to query the information about a security group.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

SecurityGroupId.description: Security group ID.

CreateTime.description: The create time.

Description.description: The description.

InnerAccessPolicy.description: Network connectivity policy within the security group.

Permissions.description: A collection of Security Group permission rules.

ResourceGroupId.description: The enterprise resource group ID where the security group resides.

SecurityGroupId.description: Security group ID.

SecurityGroupReferences.description: The complete information about the authorization of all user-specified security groups.

ServiceId.description: The virtual quotient ID corresponding to the security group.

ServiceManaged.description: Whether the owner of the security group is a cloud product or vendor.

Tags.description: The tags.

VpcId.description: Secure the group's proprietary network.

DATASOURCE::ECS::SecurityGroups.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::SecurityGroups is used to query the basic information about security groups.

NetworkType.description:
The network type of the security group. Valid values:
vpc
classic

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description:
The ID of the resource group to which the instance belongs. 
If this parameter is specified to query resources,up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SecurityGroupId.description: The ID of the security group.

SecurityGroupIds.description: The IDs of security groups. The value is a JSON array that consists of up to 100 security group IDs. Separate multiple security group IDs with commas (,).

SecurityGroupName.description: The name of the security group.

SecurityGroupType.description:
The type of the security group. Valid values:
normal: basic security group
enterprise: advanced security group
Note If you do not specify this parameter, both basic and advanced security groups are queried.

Tags.description: Tags of securitygroup.

VpcId.description: The ID of the virtual private cloud (VPC) to which the security group belongs.

SecurityGroupIds.description: The list of security group ids.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Snapshot.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Snapshot is used to query the information about a snapshot.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

CreateTime.description: The create time.

Description.description: The description.

InstantAccess.description: Whether snapshot speed availability is enabled.

ProductCode.description: The product number inherited from the mirror market.

RemainTime.description: Remaining completion time for the snapshot being created.

ResourceGroupId.description: The resource group id.

Tags.description: The tags.

Usage.description: A resource type that has a reference relationship.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Snapshots.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Snapshots is used to query all snapshots of an Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instance or a disk.

Category.description:
The category of the snapshot. Valid values:
Standard: normal snapshot
Flash: local snapshot
The local snapshot feature is replaced by the instant access feature. When you specify this parameter, take note of the following items:
 If you have used local snapshots before December 14, 2020, you can use this parameter.
 If you have not used local snapshots before December 14, 2020, you cannot use this parameter.
 Note This parameter will be removed in the future. We recommend that you use other parameters to ensure future compatibility.

Encrypted.description: Specifies whether the snapshot is encrypted. Default value: false.

KMSKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used by the data disk.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the snapshot belongs. If this parameter is specified to query resources, up to 1,000 resources that belong to the specified resource group can be displayed in the response.

SnapshotIds.description: The IDs of snapshots. The value can be a JSON array that consists of up to 100 snapshot IDs. Separate multiple snapshot IDs with commas (,).

SnapshotLinkId.description: The ID of the snapshot chain.

SnapshotType.description:
Specifies whether the snapshot has been used to create images or disks. Valid values:
auto: automatic snapshot
user: manual snapshot
all: all snapshot types

SourceDiskType.description:
The type of the source disk for which the snapshot was created. Valid values:
System: system disk
Data: data disk
Note These values are case-insensitive.

Status.description:
The status of the snapshot. Default value: all. Valid values:
progressing: The snapshot is being created.
accomplished: The snapshot is created.
failed: The snapshot fails to be created.
all: all snapshot statuses.

Tags.description: Tags of snapshot.

Usage.description:
The type of the snapshot. Default value: all. Valid values:
image: The snapshot has been used to create custom images.
disk: The snapshot has been used to create disks.
image_disk: The snapshot has been used to create custom images and data disks.
none: The snapshot has not been used to create custom images or disks.

DATASOURCE::ECS::Zones.description: DATASOURCE::ECS::Zones is used to query zones.

DataDiskCategory.description:
The category of the data disk. Valid values:
cloud: basic disk
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
ephemeral_ssd: local SSD
cloud_essd: enhanced SSD (ESSD)

InstanceChargeType.description:
The billing method of the resource. For more information, see Billing overview. Valid values:
PrePaid: subscription
PostPaid: pay-as-you-go
Default value: PostPaid.

InstanceType.description: The instance type.

IoOptimized.description:
Specifies whether the instance is I/O optimized. Valid values:
none: non-I/O optimized
optimized: I/O optimized
Default value: optimized.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:

Default is Never.

ResourceType.description:

instance: ECS instance
disk: cloud disk

ddh: dedicated host

SystemDiskCategory.description:
The category of the system disk. Valid values:
cloud: basic disk
cloud_efficiency: ultra disk
cloud_ssd: standard SSD
ephemeral_ssd: local SSD
cloud_essd: enhanced SSD (ESSD)

DATASOURCE::EHPC::Nodes.description: DATASOURCE::EHPC::Nodes is used to query nodes in an Elastic High Performance Computing (E-HPC) cluster.

HostName.description: The name of the node. You can perform a fuzzy search. MySQL regular expressions are supported.

HostNamePrefix.description: The prefix of the hostname. You can query nodes that have a specified prefix.

HostNameSuffix.description: The suffix of the hostname. You can query nodes that have a specified suffix.

PrivateIpAddress.description: The private IP address of the node.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

Role.description:
The type of the node. Valid values:
Manager: management node
Login: logon node
Compute: compute node

AllocationId.description: The ID of the EIP instance.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

AllocationId.description: The ID of the EIP instance.

Bandwidth.description: The peak bandwidth of the EIP. Unit: Mbps.

BandwidthPackageBandwidth.description: The bandwidth value of the Internet shared bandwidth added to the EIP. The unit is Mbps.

BandwidthPackageId.description: The ID of the added shared bandwidth.

BandwidthPackageType.description: The type of bandwidth. Only **CommonBandwidthPackage** (shared bandwidth) is supported.

BusinessStatus.description: The business status of the EIP instance.

DeletionProtection.description: Whether the delete protection function is turned on.

Description.description: The description of the EIP instance.

EipBandwidth.description: AN EIP is added to an Internet shared bandwidth before or exit purchase an Internet shared bandwidth instance after the bandwidth Mbps Set for the bandwidth.

HdMonitorStatus.description: Whether the second-level monitoring is enabled for the EIP.

InstanceId.description: The ID of the current bound instance.

InstanceType.description: The type of the current bound instance.

IpAddress.description: The IP address of the EIP.

PaymentType.description: The billing method of the EIP.

ReservationActiveTime.description: The effective time of the renewal fee, in the format of 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ'.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group.

DATASOURCE::EIP::Addresses.description: DATASOURCE::EIP::Addresses is used to query the information about elastic IP addresses (EIPs).

AllocationId.description: The ID of the EIP that you want to query.

InstanceId.description: The ID of the cloud resource.

InstanceType.description:
The type of the current bound instance.
- **EcsInstance**: an ECS instance of the VPC type.
- **SlbInstance**: an SLB instance of the VPC type.
- **Nat**:NAT gateway.
- **HaVip**: a highly available virtual IP address.
- **NetworkInterface**: Secondary ENI.

IpAddress.description: The IP address pool to which the EIP that you want to query belongs.

Isp.description:
The line type. Valid values:
BGP (default): BGP (Multi-ISP) lines. All regions support BGP (Multi-ISP) EIPs.
BGP_PRO: BGP (Multi-ISP) Pro lines. 
Only the following regions support BGP (Multi-ISP) Pro lines: 
China (Hong Kong), Singapore, Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), Philippines (Manila), Indonesia (Jakarta), and Thailand (Bangkok).
For more information about BGP (Multi-ISP) and BGP (Multi-ISP) Pro, see EIP line types.
If you are allowed to use single-ISP bandwidth, you can also choose one of the following values:
ChinaTelecom: China Telecom
ChinaUnicom: China Unicom
ChinaMobile: China Mobile
ChinaTelecom_L2: China Telecom L2
ChinaUnicom_L2: China Unicom L2
ChinaMobile_L2: China Mobile L2
If your services are deployed in China East 1 Finance, you must set this parameter to BGP_FinanceCloud.

PaymentType.description: The billing method of the EIP. Valid values:  PrePaid: subscription PostPaid: pay-as-you-go.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ResourceGroupId.description: The ID of the resource group to which the EIPs belong.

SegmentInstanceId.description:
The ID of the consecutive EIPs.
This parameter value is returned only if the EIP is a continuous EIP.

DATASOURCE::ENS::Image.description: DATASOURCE::ENS::Image is used to query the information about a custom image.

ImageId.description: The ID of the image. Fuzzy search is supported.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ComputeType.description: Computing type. ens_vm/ens: x86 computing. bare_metal: x86 bare machine or x86 bare metal. arm_vm: ARM computing. arm_bare_metal: ARM bare machine or ARM bare metal. pcfarm: heterogeneous computing.

CreateTime.description: The image creation time. The time follows the ISO 8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

ImageOwnerAlias.description:
The source of the image. Valid values:
- **others**: a custom image that is shared by other Alibaba Cloud accounts.
- **self**: your own custom image.

ImageSize.description: The size of the image. Unit: GiB.

InstanceId.description: The ID of the instance corresponding to the image.

Platform.description:
The type of operating system used by the image. Valid values:
- **centos**
- **ubuntu**
- **alios**
- **debian**
- **rhel**
- **windows**

SnapshotId.description: The ID of the snapshot corresponding to the image.

DATASOURCE::ENS::Instance.description: DATASOURCE::ENS::Instance is used to query the information about an Edge Node Service (ENS) instance.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

EnsRegionId.description: The node ID. When ScheduleAreaLevel is Region, EnsRegionId is required. When ScheduleAreaLevel is Big,Middle,Small, EnsRegionId is invalid.

InstanceName.description: The instance name. Example value: test-InstanceName. It must be 2 to 128 characters in length and must start with an uppercase or lowercase letter or a Chinese character. It cannot start with http:// or https. Can contain Chinese, English, numbers, half-width colons (:), underscores (_), periods (.

InstanceType.description: The specification of the instance. Example value: ens.sn1.

InternetMaxBandwidthOut.description: Maximum public network bandwidth. The field type is Long, and the precision may be lost during serialization/deserialization.

PaymentType.description: Instance payment method.

PrivateIpAddress.description: The private IP address. Can only be used for node-level scheduling. If a private IP address is specified, the number of instances can only be one, and both the private IP address and the vSwitch ID are not empty, the private IP address takes effect.

SecurityId.description: ID of the security group to which the instance belongs.

SystemDisk.description: System Disk Specification. SystemDisk is a non-required parameter when InstanceType is x86_pm,x86_bmi,x86_bm,pc_bmi, or arm_bmi. SystemDisk is a required parameter when instanceType is other specification families.

VSwitchId.description: The ID of the vSwitch to which the instance belongs.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

InstanceIds.description: The list of instance IDs.

DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingConfigurations.description: DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingConfigurations is used to query the information about scaling configurations.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ScalingConfigurationIds.description: The ID of scaling configuration that you want to query. Valid values : 1 to 10. The IDs of active and inactive scaling configurations are displayed in the query results, and can be differentiated by LifecycleState.

ScalingConfigurationNames.description: The name of scaling configuration that you want to query. Valid values : 1 to 10. The names of inactive scaling configurations are not displayed in the query results, and no error is reported.

ScalingGroupId.description: The ID of the scaling group. You can use the ID to query all scaling configurations in the scaling group.

ScalingConfigurationIds.description: The list of scaling configuration IDs.

DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingGroups.description: DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingGroups is used to query scaling groups.

GroupType.description:
The type of instances that are managed by the scaling group. Valid values:
ECS: the Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances
ECI: the elastic container instances
Default value: ECS.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ScalingGroupIds.description: The ID of scaling group that you want to query.Valid values: 1 to 20. The IDs of inactive scaling groups are not displayed in the query results, and no error is reported.

ScalingGroupNames.description: The name of scaling group that you want to query. Valid values: 1 to 20. The names of inactive scaling groups are not displayed in the query results, and no error is reported.

ScalingGroupIds.description: The list of scaling group IDs.

DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingRule.description: DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingRule is used to query the information about a scaling rule.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ScalingRuleId.description: The ID of the scaling rule that you want to query.

AdjustmentType.description: The adjustment method of the scaling rule.

AdjustmentValue.description: The number of instances that must be scaled based on the scaling rule.

Alarms.description: The event-triggered tasks that are associated with the scaling rule. The value of this parameter is returned only if you set ShowAlarmRules to true. Otherwise, null is returned.

Cooldown.description: The cooldown period of the scaling rule.

InitialMaxSize.description: The maximum number of ECS instances that can be contained in the scaling group.

MaxSize.description: The maximum number of ECS instances that can be contained in the scaling group.

MetricName.description: The name of the metric of the event-triggered task that is associated with the scaling rule.

MinAdjustmentMagnitude.description: The minimum number of instances that must be scaled.

MinSize.description: The minimum number of ECS instances that must be contained in the scaling group.

PredictiveScalingMode.description: The mode of the predictive scaling rule.

PredictiveTaskBufferTime.description: The amount of buffer time before prediction tasks are executed.

PredictiveValueBehavior.description: The action on the predicted maximum value.

PredictiveValueBuffer.description: The ratio based on which the predicted value is increased when PredictiveValueBehavior is set to PredictiveValueOverrideMaxWithBuffer.

ScaleInEvaluationCount.description: The number of consecutive times that the event-triggered task for scale-in purposes must meet the threshold conditions before an alert is triggered.

ScaleOutEvaluationCount.description: The number of consecutive times that the event-triggered task created for scale-out purposes must meet the threshold conditions before an alert is triggered.

ScalingGroupId.description: The ID of the scaling group.

ScalingRuleAri.description: The unique identifier of the scaling rule.

ScalingRuleId.description: The ID of the scaling rule.

ScalingRuleName.description: The name of the scaling rule.

ScalingRuleType.description: The type of the scaling rule.

StepAdjustments.description: The step adjustments of the step scaling rule.

TargetValue.description: The target value of the metric.

DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingRules.description: DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingRules are used to query the information about scaling rules.

RefreshOptions.description:
The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:
- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

ScalingGroupId.description: The ID of the scaling group.

ScalingRuleAris.description: The unique identifiers of the scaling rules that you want to query.

ScalingRuleIds.description: The IDs of the scaling rules that you want to query.

ScalingRuleNames.description: The names of the scaling rules that you want to query.

ScalingRuleType.description:
The type of the scaling rule. Valid values:
SimpleScalingRule: adjusts the number of ECS instances based on the values of the AdjustmentType and AdjustmentValue parameters.
TargetTrackingScalingRule: calculates the number of ECS instances that need to be scaled in a dynamic manner and maintains the value of a predefined metric close to the value of the TargetValue parameter.
StepScalingRule: scales ECS instances in steps based on the specified thresholds and metric values.
PredictiveScalingRule: uses machine learning to analyze historical monitoring data of the scaling group and predicts the future values of metrics. In addition, Auto Scaling automatically creates scheduled tasks to adjust the boundary values for the scaling group.

ShowAlarmRules.description:
Specifies whether to return the event-triggered tasks that are associated with the scaling rule. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

ScalingRuleIds.description: The list of scaling rule IDs.

DATASOURCE::Eipanycast::AnycastEipAddres.description: DATASOURCE::Eipanycast::AnycastEipAddress is used to query the information about an Anycast elastic IP address (Anycast EIP).

Bid.description: The BID of the account to which the Anycast EIP belongs.

CreateTime.description: The point in time at which the Anycast EIP was created. The time follows the ISO8601 standard in the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ format. The time is displayed in UTC.

* Associating
* Unassociating
* Allocated
* Associated
* Modifying
* Releasing
* Released

Tags.description: The tags of the EIP.

FlowId.description: The unique ID of the flow.

FlowName.description: The name of the flow. The name must be unique within a region for the same Alibaba Cloud account.

LastModifiedTime.description: The time when the flow was last modified.

RoleArn.description: The Alibaba Cloud resource name (ARN) of the authorized role on which the execution of the flow relies. During the execution of the flow, CloudFlow assumes the role to call API operations of relevant services.
